description_long: This gatehouse features a prominent arched entryway framed by two thick, layered stone and brick arches, with the outermost arch in a pale stone color and the inner arch in a reddish-brown hue. The main doors are constructed from vertically aligned dark wooden planks, giving a robust and historic appearance. Decorative stone corbels support the arch on either side, adding architectural detail and structural emphasis. The doors are double-leafed, with small, simple metal handles at the center, suggesting manual operation. The structure is solid and imposing, likely designed to control access to a larger public or institutional building. The overall design evokes a sense of security and tradition, typical of historic or civic architecture.
description: A robust gatehouse with a double wooden door set in a layered arched stone and brick entryway, featuring decorative corbels and metal handles.
description_view_0: Side profile showing the depth of the arched entry and the thickness of the gatehouse walls.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the semi-circular arch and the wooden door panels beneath.
description_view_2: Opposite side profile, mirroring the depth and layered construction of the archway.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the full arched entry, double wooden doors, corbels, and metal handles.
attribute_entryway_design: arched stone and brick doorway with double wooden doors and metal handles
attribute_window_placement: no visible windows on the structure
